rows.description: When evidence is missing, correct behavior is to request evidence or human review, not fill gaps.
When source is weak, the system must avoid validated claims and client-facing certainty.
When production/change action is requested without rollback, output must block execution.
External aggregators and community answers are candidates, not truth.
Structured data without schema should be treated as incomplete evidence.
Operational recommendations without an owner often have low executability.
High process variation may indicate rework, exception handling or policy drift.
Excessive human review may reduce usefulness when evidence and gate are clear.
Missing escalation is more dangerous when action has real-world side effects.
Anchored evidence should reduce semantic variance and unsupported claims.
Client-style output must separate observed facts, inferred risks and recommendations.
A case only creates durable value when converted into a monitoring metric or recurring ritual.
